explanation: Rule: Har term ko 3 se multiply kiya gaya hai. 567 × 3 = 1701.
Rule: Har term ko 3 se multiply kiya gaya hai (Pure Geometric ×3). 27 × 3 = 81.
Rule: Har pad 5 guna ho raha hai. 6250 × 5 = 31250.
Rule: Har term ko 2 se multiply kiya gaya hai. 444 × 2 = 888.
Rule: Har baar +29 add hota hai. 119 + 29 = 148.
Rule: Har baar +21 add hota hai. 86 + 21 = 107.
Rule: Har baar +6 add hota hai. 17 + 6 = 23.
Rule: Har baar +13 add hota hai. 93 + 13 = 106.
Rule: Differences GP mein hain (-243, -81, -27, -9), har baar ÷3. Agla diff -9÷3=-3. 265 - 3 = 262.
Rule: Differences GP mein hain (-243, -81, -27, -9), har baar ÷3. Agla diff -3. 122 - 3 = 119.
Rule: Differences double ho rahe hain (-2, -4, -8, -16). Agla diff -32. 353 - 32 = 321.
Rule: Differences ×3 ho rahe hain (-3, -9, -27, -81). Agla diff -243. 306 - 243 = 63.
Rule: Differences ×3 ho rahe hain (-11, -33, -99). Agla diff -297. 733 - 297 = 436.
Rule: Differences prime numbers hain (+7, +11, +13, +17). Agla prime +19. 93 + 19 = 112.
Rule: Differences AP mein badh rahe hain (+9, +11, +13...). Agla diff +15. 46 + 15 = 61.
Rule: Perfect squares ghata rahe hain (-1², -2², -3², -4²). Agla -5² = -25. 99 - 25 = 74.
Rule: Perfect squares ghata rahe hain (-2², -3², -4², -5²). Agla -6² = -36. 945 - 36 = 909.
Rule: Differences AP mein badh rahe hain (+27, +29, +31). Agla diff +33. 247 + 33 = 280.
Rule: Perfect squares add ho rahe hain (+16, +25, +36). Agla +7² = 49. 292 + 49 = 341.
Rule: Perfect squares add ho rahe hain (+4, +9, +16). Agla +5² = 25. 57 + 25 = 82.

Rule: Prime numbers ke cubes hain (11³, 13³, 17³, 19³). Agla prime 17 hai; 17³ = 4913.
Rule: Even numbers ke squares hain (4², 6², 8², 10², 12²...). Missing values 10² = 100 aur 12² = 144 hain.
Rule: Even numbers ke cubes hain (8³, 10³, 12³, 14³, 16³, 18³...). Agla 20³ = 8000 hai.
Rule: Prime numbers ke squares hain (11², 13², 17², 19², 23², 29²). Missing prime 19 hai; 19² = 361.

set5.word: Find the missing number: MY 27, KZ 64, ?, GB 216, EC 343
explanation: Rule: Numbers consecutive cubes hain (3³, 4³, 5³, 6³); missing number 125 hai. Letters bhi pattern follow karte hain — M(−2)→K(−2)→I aur Y(+1)→Z(+1)→A, isliye code IA125 hai.
Rule: Har number pichhle do numbers ka sum hai. 47 + 76 = 123 (check: 76+123=199).
Rule: Even position wali series (S2) ×3 hoti hai. 3×3=9, 9×3=27, 27×3=81.
Rule: S1 (odd positions): 1, 3, 5, 7. S2 (even positions): 2², 3², 4², 5². Missing 6th term = 4² = 16.
Rule: S2 (even positions) mein +4 hota hai: 129, ?, 137. Isliye 129+4=133.
Rule: S1 (odd positions) mein +4 hota hai (15,19,23,27,31,35). Agla odd position: 31+4=35.
Rule: S2 (even positions) mein +3 hota hai (33,36,39,?). Agla: 39+3=42.
Rule: S2 (even positions) mein +2 hota hai (295,297,?). Agla: 297+2=299.
Rule: S1 (odd positions) mein +22 hota hai (213,235,257,?). Agla: 257+22=279.
Rule: Pattern +5, −2 baari-baari chalta hai: 43+5=48, 48-2=46, 46+5=51, 51-2=49, 49+5=54, 54-2=52. Missing value 51 hai.

Rule: S1 (odd positions) mein +4 hota hai (67,71,75,?). Agla: 75+4=79.
Rule: Multiples of 3 add ho rahe hain (+3,+6,+9,+12,+15,+18). Agla +21. 29+21=50.
Rule: Odd numbers add ho rahe hain (+3,+5,+7,+9). Agla +7. 4+7=11.
Rule: Har term ko 3 se divide karo. 2 ÷ 3 = 2/3.
Rule: Har term ko 9 se multiply karo. (−1/9) × 9 = −1.
Rule: Har term ko 4 se divide karo. −2 ÷ 4 = −1/2.
Rule: Har term ko 2 se multiply karo. (1/88) × 2 = 1/44.
Rule: Numerator mein +9 hota hai. 41+9=50, isliye 50/9.
Rule: Numerator mein −5 hota hai. 17−5=12, isliye 12/5.
Rule: Decimal roop mein: 1.2, 1.5, 1.8, 2.1, 2.4 — har baar +0.3 hota hai. Agla: 2.4+0.3=2.7 = 27/10.

Rule: Differences AP mein badh rahe hain (−1.3,−2.6,−3.9). Agla diff −5.2. 17.1−5.2=11.9.
Rule: Differences AP mein badh rahe hain (+0.7,+1.4,+2.1). Agla diff +2.8. 17+2.8=19.8.

set8.word: Find the wrong term in the series: 1.5, 2, 3, 6, 18, 108, 1964
explanation: Rule: Har term pichhle do terms ka product hai (1.5×2=3, 2×3=6, 3×6=18, 6×18=108). Agla term 18×108=1944 hona chahiye, lekin diya gaya 1964 galat hai.

set8.word: Find the wrong term in the series: 3, 5, 7, 6, 10, 14, 12, 24, 28, 24, 40, 56
explanation: Rule: Teen-teen ke groups hain, har group pichhle group ka ×2 hai: (3,5,7)→(6,10,14)→(12,20,28). Teesra group hona chahiye 12,20,28, lekin diya gaya hai 12,24,28 — isliye 24 galat hai.

set9.word: Find the wrong term in the series: 47, 51, 57, 65, 69, 76, 83
explanation: Rule: Sahi pattern mein differences +4,+6,+8,+10,+12 hote hain: 47+4=51, 51+6=57, 57+8=65, 65+10=75, 75+12=87. Isliye 76 galat hai, hona chahiye 75.

set9.word: Find the wrong term in the series: 147, 293, 587, 1173, 2346, 4693
explanation: Rule: ×2−1 aur ×2+1 alternately hota hai: 147×2−1=293, 293×2+1=587, 587×2−1=1173, 1173×2+1=2347. Diya gaya hai 2346, isliye galat hai.

set9.word: Find the wrong term in the series: 46, 93, 188, 379, 764, 1529
Find the wrong term in the series: 49, 98, 196, 394, 784, 1568
explanation: Rule: Har term ko 2 se multiply karna hai: 196×2=392. Diya gaya hai 394, isliye galat hai.

set9.word: Find the wrong term in the series: 30, 15, 15, 22.5, 46, 112.5
explanation: Rule: Multiplier ×0.5, ×1, ×1.5, ×2, ×2.5 hai: 22.5×2=45. Diya gaya hai 46, isliye galat hai.

set9.word: Find the wrong term in the series: 14, 21, 63, 378, 3402
explanation: Rule: Multiplier ×1.5, ×3, ×6, ×12 hai: 14×1.5=21, 21×3=63, 63×6=378, 378×12=4536. Diya gaya hai 3402, isliye galat hai.

set9.word: Find the wrong term in the series: 1, 5, 17, (39), 65, 101, (145), 197
explanation: Rule: Pattern hai even number² + 1 (0²+1=1, 2²+1=5, 4²+1=17, 6²+1=37, 8²+1=65, 10²+1=101, 12²+1=145). Isliye (39) galat hai — sahi value 37 honi chahiye; (145) sahi hai.
Rule: Yeh complex alternating multiplier-cum-addition pattern hai jisse verified next term 656 milta hai (357+299=656).
Rule: Alternating cube/square add hota hai (+1³,+2²,+3³,+4²,+5³,+6²). 195 + 6² = 195+36 = 231.

set10.word: Find the missing term: CHO, DIP, EJQ, ?
explanation: Rule: Har letter mein +1 hota hai. E+1=F, J+1=K, Q+1=R → FKR.

set10.word: Find the missing term: OTE, PUF, QVG, RWH, ?
explanation: Rule: Har letter mein +1 hota hai. R+1=S, W+1=X, H+1=I → SXI.

set10.word: Find the missing term: RJO, SKP, TLQ, ?
explanation: Rule: Har letter mein +1 hota hai. T+1=U, L+1=M, Q+1=R → UMR.

set10.word: Find the missing term: HAF, KDI, NGL, QJO, ?
explanation: Rule: Har letter mein +3 hota hai. Q+3=T, J+3=M, O+3=R → TMR.

set10.word: Find the missing term: CNT, FQU, ITV, LWW, ?
explanation: Rule: Pehle do letters mein +3 aur teesre mein +1 hota hai. L+3=O, W+3=Z, W+1=X → OZX.

set10.word: Find the missing term: ADE, CFH, EHK, GJN, ?
explanation: Rule: +2, +2, +3 pattern hai. G+2=I, J+2=L, N+3=Q → ILQ.

set10.word: Find the missing term: DFA, HJE, LNI, PRM, ?
explanation: Rule: Har letter mein +4 hota hai. P+4=T, R+4=V, M+4=Q → TVQ.

set10.word: Find the missing term: GDJ, KHN, OLR, SPV, ?
explanation: Rule: Har letter mein +4 hota hai. S+4=W, P+4=T, V+4=Z → WTZ.

set11.word: Find the missing term: BPR, FRV, JTZ, NVD, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +4. Position 2: +2. Position 3: +4. N+4=R, V+2=X, D+4=H → RXH.

set11.word: Find the missing term: G, Z, I, X, K, V, ?, ?
explanation: Rule: S1: +2 (G,I,K,M...). S2: −2 (Z,X,V,T...). Agle terms: M aur T.

set11.word: Find the missing term: AM, DK, GI, JG, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +3 (A,D,G,J,M). Position 2: −2 (M,K,I,G,E). Agla: M aur E → ME.

set11.word: Find the missing term: Mnl, kpj, irh, ?
Find the missing term: GEA, IFB, LHA, QKB, XOA, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: prime gaps (+2,+3,+5,+7,+11). Position 2: +1,+2,+3,+4,+5. Position 3: A,B alternate. X(24)+11=35(I), O(15)+5=20(T), A→B → ITB.

set11.word: Find the missing term: TJV, WFY, ZBB, CXE, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +3. Position 2: −4. Position 3: +3. C+3=F, X−4=T, E+3=H → FTH.

set11.word: Find the missing term: FQN, CLL, ZGJ, WBH, ?
Find the missing term: MIT, JEQ, GAN, DWK, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: −3. Position 2: −4. Position 3: −3. D−3=A, W−4=S, K−3=H → ASH.

set11.word: Find the missing term: MFX, JCU, GZR, DWO, ?
explanation: Rule: Har letter mein −3 hota hai. D−3=A, W−3=T, O−3=L → ATL.

set11.word: Find the missing term: DRH, YOG, TLF, OIE, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: −5. Position 2: −3. Position 3: −1. O−5=J, I−3=F, E−1=D → JFD.

set12.word: Find the missing term: JAS, HDV, FGY, DJB, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: −2. Position 2: +3. Position 3: +3. D−2=B, J+3=M, B+3=E → BME.

set12.word: Find the missing term: CMW, MWG, WGQ, GQA, ?
explanation: Rule: Agla term pichhle term ke last do letters se shuru hota hai, +10 wrap ke saath. Pichhla GQA hai, agla QA se shuru hota hai; A(1)+10=K(11) → QAK.

set12.word: Find the missing term: ABZ, BCY, CDX, DEW, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1&2: consecutive forward. Position 3: consecutive backward. Agla: E, F, V → EFV.

set12.word: Find the missing term: A, C, E, D, C, E, G, F, ?
explanation: Rule: Group logic hai +2,+2,−1,−1,+2,+2,−1... last group mein E se −1 → E.

set12.word: Find the missing term: EGI, KMO, QSU, ?
explanation: Rule: Har letter mein +6 hota hai, U+6 wrap karke A ban jaata hai. Q+6=W, S+6=Y, U+6=A → WYA.

set12.word: Find the missing term: YZ, BCD, FGHI, ?, QRSTUV
explanation: Rule: Group ka size badhta hai (2,3,4,5,6); pehle letter ka gap +3,+4,+5,+6 hota hai. F+5=K; group of 5 K se shuru hota hai → KLMNO.

set12.word: Find the missing term: FPW, FFPPPWW, FFFFPPPPPWWW, ?
explanation: Rule: F ka count double hota hai (1,2,4,8), P ka count odd numbers hai (1,3,5,7), W ka count consecutive hai (1,2,3,4). Agla group 8 F, 7 P, 4 W ka hota hai.

set12.word: Find the missing term: aaabcccccccc, aaabbcccc, aaabbbbcc, ?
explanation: Rule: 'a' hamesha 3 rehta hai, 'c' aadha hota jaata hai (8,4,2,1), 'b' double hota jaata hai (1,2,4,8) → 3 a's, 8 b's, 1 c.

set12.word: Find the missing term: QUESTION, USINETO, SNTIEO, NIOTE, ?
explanation: Rule: Har step mein original word ke first aur last letters remove hote hain, aur baaki letters swap hote hain. N aur E remove karne par → ITOE.

set12.word: Find the missing term: UE 88, VG 84, WI 80, XK 76, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +1. Position 2: +2. Number: −4. X+1=Y, K+2=M, 76−4=72 → YM72.

set13.word: Find the missing term: VO 39, TM 35, RK 31, PI 27, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: −2. Position 2: −2. Number: −4. P−2=N, I−2=G, 27−4=23 → NG23.

set13.word: Find the missing term: XF 11, YI 15, ZL 19, AO 23, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +1. Position 2: +3. Number: +4. A+1=B, O+3=R, 23+4=27 → BR27.

set13.word: Find the missing term: FM 61, EK 58, DI 55, CG 52, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: −1. Position 2: −2. Number: −3. C−1=B, G−2=E, 52−3=49 → BE49.

set13.word: Find the missing term: AH 21, CJ 18, EL 15, GN 12, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +2. Position 2: +2. Number: −3. G+2=I, N+2=P, 12−3=9 → IP9.

set13.word: Find the missing term: PJ 16, SM 25, VP 36, YS 49, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +3. Position 2: +3. Number: consecutive squares (4²,5²,6²,7²,8²). Y+3=B, S+3=V, 8²=64 → BV64.

set13.word: Find the missing term: YL 93, XK 88, WJ 83, VI 78, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: −1. Position 2: −1. Number: −5. V−1=U, I−1=H, 78−5=73 → UH73.

set13.word: Find the missing term: NW 17, PY 19, RA 21, TC 23, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +2. Position 2: +2. Number: +2. T+2=V, C+2=E, 23+2=25 → VE25.

set13.word: Find the missing term: MY 84, KW 80, IU 76, GS 72, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: −2. Position 2: −2. Number: −4. G−2=E, S−2=Q, 72−4=68 → EQ68.

set13.word: Find the missing term: HM 71, JP 68, LS 65, NV 62, ?
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +2. Position 2: +3. Number: −3. N+2=P, V+3=Y, 62−3=59 → PY59.

set13.word: Find the missing term: B 2, C 3, E 5, G 7, ?, M 13, Q 17
explanation: Rule: Numbers prime sequence hain (2,3,5,7,11,13,17...). Letter alphabet mein us number ki position par hota hai — 11th letter K hai → K11.

set14.word: Find the missing term: BV 101, ?, DX 109, EY 113, FZ 117
Find the missing term: EZ 10, ?, ID 36, KF 49, MH 62
explanation: Rule: Position 1: +2. Position 2: +2. Number: +13. E+2=G, Z+2=B, 10+13=23 → GB23.

set14.word: Fill in the blanks: _ C _ W _ _ _ C _ W _ _
explanation: Rule: Poori sequence CCCWWW baar-baar repeat hoti hai. Blanks bharne par milta hai → WWCWWWCW.

set14.word: Fill in the blanks: _ O _ O _ _ O _ P _ O _
explanation: Rule: Sequence POOP baar-baar repeat hoti hai. Blanks bharne par → OPOPOOP.

set14.word: Fill in the blanks: Z _ A _ Z _ _ Z A _ Z _
explanation: Rule: Sequence ZZAAZZ repeat hoti hai. Blanks bharne par → ZZAZZA.

set14.word: Fill in the blanks: Y _ Y _ _ Y _ T _ Y _ Y
explanation: Rule: Sequence YTTTYY repeat hoti hai. Blanks bharne par → TYTYYT.

set14.word: Fill in the blanks: REMR RE_R_EEMRRE_M_
explanation: Rule: Group of 4 'REMR' baar-baar repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → MREM.
Rule: NANAB block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → ANBAN.
Rule: PAAZ VAAZ block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → ZVPAPZAV.
Rule: DFHIJO block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → HFDHF.

set15.word: Fill in the blanks: f h_h_g f_h g_h_f f_g_h_f
options: g h f h g f f g
g h f h g h f g
g h f f g h f g
g h f h g h h g
explanation: Sahi Jawaab: g h f h g h h g
Rule: fhghgf block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → g h f h g h h g.
Rule: MNQOJK block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → QNMOJ.
Rule: QUANTUM word repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → NUMTA.
Rule: MEDIC block cyclic roop mein repeat hota hai (MEDIC/MEDIG/MEDIH). Blanks bharne par → DCFMI.
Rule: fdde/efdd blocks alternate ya shift hote hain. Blanks bharne par → ddfeedee.
Rule: ABACAB block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → BBACBAB.
Rule: KLPQS block repeat/shift hota hai. Blanks bharne par → KPLPQLPSKP.

set15.word: Fill in the blanks: P Q _ S _ P P Q P _ A P P Q _ S A P _ Q
explanation: Rule: PQPSA block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → PASPP.

set15.word: Fill in the blanks: q p _ r _ r q _ p _ r p _ q _ p r _ p _ q
options: r p q r r p r q
r p q r r q r r
p q r r p q r r
q r p r r q p r
explanation: Sahi Jawaab: r p q r r q r r
Rule: qprr block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → r p q r r q r r.

set15.word: Fill in the blanks: _ _ C _ D C _ _ C C _ _
explanation: Rule: CCDDC block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → CDCCDDC.

set16.word: Fill in the blanks: S _ Y A _ F I K M _ S U W
explanation: Rule: Growing gap pattern hai (gaps 2 letters se badhte hain). Blanks bharne par → VCP.

set16.word: Fill in the blanks: X _ _ Y_X X _ _ X _ _
options: X X X Y X X Y
X X Y Y A A X
X Y X Y X X Y
Y X X Y X X X
explanation: Sahi Jawaab: X X X Y X X Y
Rule: XXYYXX block repeat hota hai. Blanks bharne par → X X X Y X X Y.

First differences: 150-?=?, 183-150=33, 219-183=36, 258-219=39. So the differences are 30, 33, 36, 39 -- increasing by 3 each time (2nd difference = 3, constant). So missing first term = 150-30 = 120.

set17.word: One number is not like the others: 1331, 2197, 3375, 4096
explanation: All numbers should be cubes of consecutive ODD numbers: 11^3=1331, 13^3=2197, 15^3=3375, 17^3=4913. But 4096 = 16^3, an EVEN cube, breaking the odd-number pattern. So 4096 is the odd one out.

set17.word: What should come at the place of question mark? 3, 4, 13, 14, 45, 46, ?
explanation: This is two interleaved series. Odd-position terms: 3, 13, 45, ... follow the rule (previous x3) + increasing add-on (4, 6, 8...): 3x3+4=13, 13x3+6=45, 45x3+8=143. Even-position terms (4, 14, 46...) are simply (odd-position term)+1. So the 7th term = 143.
Each term is obtained by multiplying the previous term by 0.8 (i.e., 4/5): 120x0.8=96, 96x0.8=76.8, 76.8x0.8=61.44, 61.44x0.8=49.152.

set17.word: What should come in place of the question mark in: 4, 6, 9, 13.5, ?, 30.375
explanation: Each term is 1.5 times the previous term (a geometric progression with ratio 3/2): 4x1.5=6, 6x1.5=9, 9x1.5=13.5, 13.5x1.5=20.25, 20.25x1.5=30.375. So the missing term is 20.25.
Look at the differences: 4, 9, 19, 39. Each difference follows d(next) = 2xd(previous)+1: 4x2+1=9, 9x2+1=19, 19x2+1=39, 39x2+1=79. So the next term = 74+79 = 153.
First differences: 5, 10, 17, 26. Second differences: 5, 7, 9 (odd numbers, increasing by 2). Next second-difference = 11, so next first-difference = 26+11 = 37, and next term = 61+37 = 98.
These are factorials: 24=4!, 120=5!, 720=6!, 5040=7!, 40320=8!. So the next term is 9! = 362880.

set18.word: What will come at the place of question mark? 1, 9, 31, 73, 141, ?
explanation: First differences: 8, 22, 42, 68. Second differences: 14, 20, 26 (increasing by 6). Next second-difference = 32, so next first-difference = 68+32 = 100, and the next term = 141+100 = 241.

set18.word: What will come at the place of question mark? 1, 2, 6, 21, 88, ?

explanation: Notice the multipliers: 6/3=2, 18/6=3, 108/18=6. These multipliers follow: each new multiplier = product of previous two multipliers (2x3=6). So next multiplier = 3x6=18, giving 108x18 = 1944.
Differences should double each time: 4, 8, 16, 32. So the series should be 25, 29, 37, 53, 85. The differences given are 4, 8, 16, 34 -- so 87 is wrong; it should be 85.

This is two interleaved series. Odd-position terms (4, 14, 34, 74) follow x2+6: 4x2+6=14, 14x2+6=34, 34x2+6=74 -- consistent. Even-position terms (5, 14, ?, 68) should follow x2+4: 5x2+4=14, 14x2+4=32, 32x2+4=68 -- consistent. So the correct 3rd even-term should be 32, not 36. Hence 36 is wrong.

set19.word: Find the WRONG term in the series: 5, 10, 15, 26, 37, 50
explanation: The intended series is n^2+1 for n=2,3,4,5,6,7: 5,10,17,26,37,50. The given series has 15 in place of 17, so 15 is the wrong term.
Rule: a(n+1) = n x a(n) + n. Check: 6x1+1=7, 7x2+2=16, 16x3+3=51, 51x4+4=208. The series shows 210 instead of 208, so 210 is wrong.
First differences: 3, 5, 9, 15. Second differences: 2, 4, 6 (increasing by 2). Next second-difference = 8, next first-difference = 15+8 = 23, so next term = 45+23 = 68.
Differences should increase by 7 each time: 7, 14, 21, 28. So the series should be 25, 32, 46, 67, 95. Since 97 appears instead of 95, 97 is the wrong term.

The differences should decrease (become more negative) by 10 each time: -90, -80, -70, -60, -50. The series gives -90, -80, -72, -58, -50. Working it through, the term 94 breaks this pattern -- it should be 96 (166-70=96, and 96-60=36 fits the rest perfectly). So 94 is wrong.
From 36 onward the series clearly doubles each time (36, 72, 144, 288). Extending backward, 9 should double to 18, not 17. So 17 is the wrong term (it should be 18).

set19.word: In this series, a wrong number is given: 1, 4, 16, 64, 324
explanation: This is a simple x4 geometric progression: 1, 4, 16, 64, 256. The last term should be 256, not 324. So 324 is wrong.

set19.word: Identify the pattern: 111x222, 222x333, 333x444, ______. Which fits next?
explanation: Each term uses consecutive multiples of 111 (111, 222, 333, 444, 555) paired with the next one, so the pattern continues as 444x555.
Rule: a(n+1) = n x a(n) + n (same family as a(n+1)=n(a(n)+1)). Check: 4x1+1=5, 5x2+2=12, 12x3+3=39, 39x4+4=160. The series shows 163 instead of 160, so 163 is wrong.
Rule: each term = (previous term x 3) - 2. Check: 12x3-2=34, 34x3-2=100, 100x3-2=298, 298x3-2=892, 892x3-2=2674. Since the series shows 894 instead of 892, 894 is the wrong term.

set20.word: Find the number that can replace the question mark: 20, 62, 189, 571, 1718, ?

explanation: This is a simple x3 geometric progression: 2, 6, 18, 54, 162, 486. The last term should be 486, not 325. So 325 is the odd one out.

set20.word: What should come at the place of question mark? 13, 16, 32, 35, 70, ?
The following series has one missing number: 8, 32, 72, 128, ?
Find the next element that replaces the ?: 3, 22, 117, 592, ?
Find the next element that replaces the ?: 10, 41, 196, 971, ?

explanation: First differences: 19, 37, 61. Second differences: 18, 24 (increasing by 6). Next second-difference = 30, next first-difference = 61+30 = 91, so next term = 123+91 = 214.

Each term has the form 1, n, n, n... n, 1 where the digit n (matching the term's position: 2,3,4,5,...) repeats one more time than the previous term, flanked by 1s: 1-2-2-1, 1-3-3-3-1, 1-4-4-4-4-1, 1-5-5-5-5-5-1, so next is 1-6-6-6-6-6-6-1 = 16666661.

set21.word: A term is missing. Complete the series: 11, 36, 147, ?, 1779, 5340, 21363
A term is missing. Complete the series: 10, ?, 36, 69, 134, 263, 520, 1033
explanation: From the 3rd term onward, the differences are 33, 65, 129, 257, 513 -- each is (a power of 2)+1: 32+1, 64+1, 128+1, 256+1, 512+1. The difference before that should be 16+1=17, so the missing term = 36-17 = 19.
Each term = (previous term x 3) - an increasing amount (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6): 14x3-1=41, 41x3-2=121, 121x3-3=360, 360x3-4=1076, 1076x3-5=3223, 3223x3-6=9663. So the missing term is 3223.

The first numbers in each pair are consecutive primes: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13. The second number = (first)^2+1: 2^2+1=5, 3^2+1=10, 5^2+1=26, 7^2+1=50, 11^2+1=122, 13^2+1=170. So the missing pair is (11, 122).
First differences: 14, 70, 350 -- each is 5 times the previous (a GP with ratio 5). Next difference = 350x5 = 1750, so next term = 435+1750 = 2185.

set21.word: Monthly savings were 4000, 4400, 4840, and 5324. Estimate the savings in the sixth month if the same trend continues.
explanation: Each month's savings is 10% more than the previous (a 1.1x growth): 4000->4400->4840->5324->5856.4->6442.04. So the 6th month's savings is approximately 6442.

set21.word: Which number will come in place of the question mark? 4, 10, 32, 130, ?
explanation: Each term is obtained by multiplying by an increasing number (2, 3, 4, 5) and adding 2: 4x2+2=10, 10x3+2=32, 32x4+2=130, 130x5+2=652.

set22.word: What will come at the place of question mark? 3, 10, 29, 66, ?, 218
What will come at the place of question mark? 1, 4, 11, 26, 57, ?
explanation: Each term = (previous term x2) + an increasing amount (2, 3, 4, 5, 6): 1x2+2=4, 4x2+3=11, 11x2+4=26, 26x2+5=57, 57x2+6=120.

set22.word: What will come at the place of question mark? 2, 6, 20, 70, ?, 924
explanation: These are central binomial coefficients, C(2n, n): C(2,1)=2, C(4,2)=6, C(6,3)=20, C(8,4)=70, C(10,5)=252, C(12,6)=924.

set22.word: What will come at the place of question mark? 2, 9, 28, 65, 126, ?

explanation: First differences: 1, 4, 9, 16 -- these are perfect squares (1^2, 2^2, 3^2, 4^2). Next difference = 5^2 = 25, so the next term = 32+25 = 57.

set22.word: What will come at the place of question mark: 4, 18, 48, 100, ?
explanation: First differences: 14, 30, 52. Second differences: 16, 22 (increasing by 6). Next second-difference = 28, next first-difference = 52+28 = 80, so the next term = 100+80 = 180.
Each term = (previous term x2) + an increasing amount (0, 1, 2, 3): 5x2+0=10, 10x2+1=21, 21x2+2=44, 44x2+3=91.
Each term = (previous term x2) + an increasing odd amount (1, 3, 5, 7): 4x2+1=9, 9x2+3=21, 21x2+5=47, 47x2+7=101.

set23.word: Select the correct option that will complete the series: (2, 7), (3, 13), (5, 31), ?, (11, 133)
explanation: The first numbers are consecutive primes: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11. The second number = n^2+n+1: 4+2+1=7, 9+3+1=13, 25+5+1=31, 49+7+1=57, 121+11+1=133. So the missing pair is (7, 57).

set23.word: Select the correct option that will complete the series: (2, 3), (5, 24), (8, 63), ?, (14, 195)
explanation: The first numbers increase by 3 each time: 2, 5, 8, 11, 14. The second number = n^2-1: 4-1=3, 25-1=24, 64-1=63, 121-1=120, 196-1=195. So the missing pair is (11, 120).

set23.word: What number will be present in place of the question mark? 5, 24, 61, 122, 213, ?
explanation: First differences: 19, 37, 61, 91. Second differences: 18, 24, 30 (increasing by 6). Next second-difference = 36, next first-difference = 91+36 = 127, so the next term = 213+127 = 340.
The differences alternate between +36 and +78: 235+36=271, 271+78=349, 349+36=385, 385+78=463, 463+36=499. So the missing term is 463.

set23.word: Find the next element that replaces the ? Logically: 7, 24, 58, 126, ?
Find the next element that replaces the ? Logically: 9, 28, 66, 142, ?
Find the next element that replaces the ? Logically: 10, 21, 54, 153, ?
